examples.subject: Re: sn of g43
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Tzvi, good morning!

Thanks for your patience as I worked on this. 

After taking a look at the plans, I believe on low frequency bands (like B13 for example) 1 antenna will be enough to cover each floor, but it is unlikely to be the case for higher frequency bands.

The G41 can boost 2 bands simultaneously, as long as they are available outside where the donor antenna is placed.

Low frequency bands are great for phone calls and SMS messaging, but offer a reduced internet speed. Higher frequency bands cannot penetrate walls very well but offer higher inernet speed.

Please let me know if that makes sense or you have any questions!

Regards,

sopMatches.title: Intake Scripts
sopMatches.excerpt:
Intake Scripts

When doing intake with a lead or customer that will eventually be handed off or routed to another team or team member, it's important to have a brief but valuable discussion about the most relevant topics. The following scripts follow our general conversation flows and should be seen purely as a guide, every conversation is different, but be sure to capture the relevant points each time. 

💡 Use the following scripts to loosely guide your intake conversations and keep them focused. 

Sales Intake

For consumer leads (<15k Sq ft) 

When doing intake for consumer leads, it’s imp

sopMatches.title: Intake, Qualifying & Scoping
sopMatches.excerpt:
Intake, Qualifying & Scoping

Once we've taken a commercial project in, we will always do extensive scoping before making recommendations or moving to design. There are a few reasons for this: 

To ensure we fully understand the project 
To allow us to guarantee a certain amount of performance 
To protect our design resources 
To build a strong relationship with the customer 

This stage belongs entirely to the signal specialist, and notes must be made diligently in PipeDrive. Any uncertainties around the design requirements should be taken up with the design team before handing off for design

examples.subject: Re: sn of g43
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Tzvi, good morning!

Sorry got your message after I left the office yesterday. I just looked at the reading and they look usable, however the signal strength is too low for a 65 or even a 75 dB booster. For those readings I think the best bet would be a 100 dB gain booster from Cel-Fi; either the all carrier G43 or the single carrier G41.

For reference; for a 75 dB gain booster we would be looking for a signal with RSRP better than around -85 dBm.

Please let me know if you have any questions or if you'd like to hop on a call any time today.

Best regards,

sopMatches.title: Vetting and Referring Installers for Commercial Projects
sopMatches.relativePath: knowledge\approved\sops\Vetting and Referring Installers for Commercial Pr 1bfcbeb1d73b81b8ae8bf11e272b9e00.md
sopMatches.excerpt:
Vetting and Referring Installers for Commercial Projects

I think these calls generally need to be more of a casual conversation, and will change with each project and each installer, but there are definitely some key points that need to be addressed. Worth keeping in mind too that we'll want to have a more detailed call with whatever installer we pick for each project, closer to the project beginning.

This call's purpose should be purely to gather info so we can pick our preferred installer from a shortlist. I imagine we'll do this with at least handful of installers for every project. At le

examples.subject: Re: sn of g43
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Tzvi,

Thanks again for your time over the phone!

Here's what we disussed for that ~5,000 sq ft office:

(A) Cel-Fi G43: ~$7,000 for all hardware components

(B) Verizon 4G LTE Network Extender 3 for Enterprise: ~$4,000 for all hardware components

(C) Verizon 5G Network Extender 4 for Enterprise: ~7,500 for all hardware components

WeBoost options: (low gain)

(A) weBoost Office 200 - $1,699.99 (without add-ons)
(B) weBoost Installed | Office 200 - $3,799.99
(C) weBoost Installed | Office 300 - $3,799.99

Please let me know if you have any questions at all!

Best regards,

sopMatches.title: Estimation
sopMatches.excerpt:
Estimation

We will always provide a potential customer with a budgetary estimate document. The client can then take that to their stakeholders for approval while preventing any surprises for the customer in terms cost. Further the estimate document provides an explanation for the commercial process with a timeline and a quote for design. 

Preparation 

1. Login to PandaDocs at 
login details are shared in LastPass
2. Access the Commercial cost estimate template
In the left hand navigation menu, navigate to Templates Waveform Commercial Cost Estimate and Design Booking [TEMPLATE]
Please do no
